1.q: Northern analysis revealed that efficient transcriptional activation of ena1 in F. oxysporum required the presence of high Na+ concentrations and alkaline ambient pH and was dependent on PacC function.

2.q: We propose a model in which PacC controls ion homeostasis in F. oxysporum at a high pH by activating expression of ena1 coordinately with a second Na+-responsive signaling pathway.

3.q: We propose a model in which PacC controls ion homeostasis in F. oxysporum at a high pH by activating expression of ena1.
